entry.title: [MK] Is Christopher Nolan The Greatest Director Working Today? | The Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Christopher Nolan is one of the most celebrated filmmakers of all time, but has he ever made a bad movie? With The Odyssey on the horizon, we're ranking Christopher Nolan's entire filmography—from Memento and The Prestige to The Dark Knight, Inception, Interstellar, Dunkirk, Tenet, Oppenheimer, and more—to decide where each film belongs.
In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we discuss Nolan's evolution as a filmmaker, revisit his biggest hits, debate his most divisive movies, share our hottest Christopher Nolan takes, and look ahead to The Odyssey.
Is Nolan the greatest blockbuster director working today?
Let us know your Christopher Nolan ranking in the comments!
Episode Timestamps:
00:00 - Intro 01:51 - Why Christopher Nolan Was Once Underrated 02:38 - Ranking Christopher Nolan's Movies 04:41 - Memento & Insomnia 07:44 - Batman Begins 09:03 - The Prestige 10:33 - The Dark Knight 12:04 - Inception 13:38 - The Dark Knight Rises 15:00 - Interstellar 16:48 - Dunkirk 20:36 - Tenet 22:18 - Oppenheimer 24:50 - Why The Odyssey Could Be Nolan's Biggest Movie Yet 27:44 - Christopher Nolan Hot Takes 30:12 - Christopher Nolan Trivia 30:56 - Is Nolan The Last Director Who Creates Movie Events? 32:42 - Christopher Nolan FMK 34:06 - Guess The Christopher Nolan Movie
#christophernolan #theodyssey #movie

entry.title: [MK] The Odyssey | Movie Review
entry.summary:
Christopher Nolan returns with The Odyssey, an epic adaptation of Homer's legendary tale, and we're breaking down whether it lives up to the massive hype. In this spoiler-free review, we discuss Matt Damon's incredible lead performance, the jaw-dropping practical filmmaking, the stunning IMAX visuals, Christopher Nolan's direction, and why this might be one of the greatest theatrical experiences in years.
Is The Odyssey Nolan's next masterpiece? How does it compare to Oppenheimer, Interstellar, The Dark Knight, and his other films? We also talk about the performances from Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, Zendaya, Robert Pattinson, John Bernthal, Elliot Page, Lupita Nyong'o, Samantha Morton, Corey Hawkins, Hamish Patel, and more.
If you're wondering whether The Odyssey is worth seeing in theaters, we've got you covered.
What did you think of The Odyssey? Let us know in the comments!
Odyssey Review Timestamps: 0:00 Is The Odyssey Christopher Nolan's best yet? 0:18 First reactions: A true cinematic event 2:07 Story, pacing & adapting Homer's epic 5:17 Cast highlights & standout performances 6:31 Why this demands the biggest screen possible 7:02 Nick's full review & technical achievements 10:00 Adaptation choices & Nolan's filmmaking 11:14 Final verdict: See it in theaters
#theodyssey #theodysseymovie #christophernolan #moviereview

entry.title: [MK] Is Evil Dead Horror's Greatest Franchise? | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
With Evil Dead Burn now in theaters, we're looking back at every movie in the legendary Evil Dead franchise—from Sam Raimi's original classic to Evil Dead II, Army of Darkness, Evil Dead (2013), Evil Dead Rise, and Ash vs Evil Dead. From Sam Raimi's groundbreaking original to Bruce Campbell's unforgettable Ash Williams, we discuss why Evil Dead remains one of the most beloved horror franchises ever made.
We also discuss Sam Raimi's influence on modern filmmaking, the evolution of the Deadites, our favorite Evil Dead movies, and what we're hoping to see next from the franchise.
What's your favorite Evil Dead movie? Let us know in the comments!!
Episode Timestamps: 00:00 - Intro 02:58 - Is The Evil Dead Still Scary? 09:35 - Why Evil Dead II Is A Horror Classic 19:18 - Army of Darkness Is Completely Different 31:04 - Was Evil Dead (2013) The Best Remake? 40:23 - Ash vs Evil Dead Deserved More Seasons 43:52 - Is Evil Dead Rise One Of The Best Horror Sequels? 50:26 - We Pitch Our Own Evil Dead Movie
#evildead #horror #movies

entry.title: [MK] The New DCU: What's Working, What's Not, And What's Next? | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
The DCU is officially underway. With Superman launching the new DC Universe and Supergirl arriving next, we're breaking down James Gunn's DCU, what's working, what isn't, and where DC Studios should go from here.
This week on The Movie Knights Roundtable, we discuss the future of the DCU after Superman, Supergirl, Clayface, Creature Commandos, Peacemaker Season 2, Lanterns, and the projects that have already been announced.
We look back at James Gunn's original DC Studios announcement, evaluate what we've seen so far, discuss whether the current strategy is working, and debate what the next phase of the DC Universe should look like.
We also discuss whether DC has become more creatively exciting than Marvel, how important Supergirl and Clayface are to the future of the franchise, and what DC Studios needs to stop doing if they want to build a successful shared universe.
If you're a fan of DC Comics, Superman, Supergirl, Batman, Wonder Woman, the Justice League, the DCU, James Gunn, Peter Safran, and the future of superhero movies, this episode is for you.
What should be the next major DCU project after Supergirl?
Episode Timestamps: 00:00 - Intro 01:35 - The Future Of The DCU 01:56 - Revisiting James Gunn's Original DCU Announcement 04:12 - Every DCU Project So Far 05:10 - Is The DCU Strategy Actually Working? 07:18 - What DC Studios Should Do Next 11:33 - DCU Canon Needs To Be More Clear 14:00 - Will Supergirl And Clayface Decide The DCU's Future? 16:49 - Is DC More Exciting Than Marvel Right Now? 18:15 - Blockbuster Or Street-Level? DC Character Game 20:48 - DCU Pitch Meeting: Greenlight Or Pass?
🎬 More from The Movie Knights: https://linktr.ee/Movie_Knights

entry.title: [MK] Supergirl | Movie Review
entry.summary:
Supergirl Review! We break down the latest DCU movie starring Millie Alcock as Kara Zor-El / Supergirl and Jason Momoa as Lobo. Does Supergirl continue the momentum from Superman and expand James Gunn's new DC Universe, or does it fall short of expectations?
In this review, we discuss Millie Alcock's performance, Jason Momoa's debut as Lobo, the movie's connection to Superman, the DCU world-building, the action sequences, visual effects, pacing issues, Craig Gillespie's direction, and whether Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow successfully adapts the beloved comic story.
Is Supergirl another win for DC Studios? Let us know your thoughts in the comments!
Supergirl Review Timestamps: 00:00 Supergirl Review & First Reactions 00:28 Millie Alcock, Lobo & Standout Performances 02:04 DCU World-Building, Action & Visuals 02:53 Pacing Problems & Story Issues 04:30 Woman of Tomorrow Adaptation Discussion 05:20 Why Supergirl Is a Fun Mess 06:25 Lobo, Villains & Supporting Characters 08:39 What Supergirl Means for the Future of the DCU 10:01 Wonder Woman Comparison 11:23 Final Verdict & Rating
#supergirl #supergirlwomanoftomorrow #dcu #dcuniverse #moviereview #comicbookmovies

In this review, we discuss Hugh Jackman's performance as Robin Hood, Jodie Comer's standout supporting role, Michael Sarnoski's direction, the film's slow-burn character study approach, pacing issues, cinematography, themes, and whether The Death of Robin Hood delivers on its ambitious premise.

entry.title: [MK] Movie Theaters Are Broken and Here's Why | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Movie theaters are facing more challenges than ever. Ticket prices keep rising, ads before movies seem longer every year, theatrical windows have shrunk, and bad audience behavior can completely ruin the experience.
This week on The Movie Knights Roundtable, we're tackling a big question: How do we fix movie theaters?
We discuss the biggest issues facing theaters today, from disruptive audiences and expensive concessions to the need for movies to feel like true events again. We also debate whether longer theatrical windows could help bring audiences back and what theater chains should be doing differently.
If you love going to the movies, this is the conversation for you.
What would you change about the movie theater experience?

entry.title: [MK] Why Steven Spielberg Is The Greatest Director Ever | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Steven Spielberg is one of the most influential filmmakers in movie history. With classics like Jaws, Jurassic Park, Raiders of the Lost Ark, E.T., Schindler's List, Saving Private Ryan, and more, Spielberg helped define modern blockbuster filmmaking.
In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we celebrate Steven Spielberg's incredible career ahead of the release of Disclosure Day. We discuss our favorite Spielberg movies, his greatest strengths as a filmmaker, the films that don't work for us, and why his impact on Hollywood is still being felt today.
Whether you're a lifelong Spielberg fan or just discovering his movies, join us for a deep dive into one of the most important filmmakers ever.

entry.title: [MK] These Movies Should Have Been TV Shows | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Some movies have amazing ideas… but would they have worked better as TV shows? In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we discuss movies that may have benefited from a longer format, including The Maze Runner, The Truman Show, Eternals, The Hitchhiker’s Guide to the Galaxy, The Running Man, The Nice Guys, and more.
We break down which movies needed more time for world-building, character development, or storytelling and why certain concepts may work better as streaming series instead of feature films.
If you love movie discussions, TV debates, adaptations, or film podcasts, this episode is for you.

entry.title: [MK] Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu | Movie Review
entry.summary:
The Mandalorian & Grogu review! We break down the newest The Mandalorian & Grogu adventure and discuss how it compares to modern Star Wars movies like Star Wars: The Last Jedi and the Disney+ era.
Pedro Pascal returns as Din Djarin alongside Grogu in Lucasfilm’s big-screen continuation of The Mandalorian. We discuss the action, emotional moments, Star Wars lore, performances, and whether the franchise successfully makes the jump from streaming to theaters.
We also dive into the movie’s place in modern Star Wars, its connections to the larger galaxy, and how it stacks up against recent Star Wars films and shows.
If you love Star Wars reviews, Mandalorian discussions, movie reviews, and blockbuster breakdowns, you’re in the right place.

entry.title: [MK] Marvel Before the MCU Was INSANE | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Before the MCU changed superhero movies forever, Marvel had one of the weirdest and most fascinating movie eras in Hollywood. In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we revisit the pre-MCU Marvel movies, from Blade and X-Men to Spider-Man, Daredevil, Punisher, Fantastic Four, Ghost Rider, and more.
We talk about the highs, the disasters, the underrated gems, and how these early Marvel movies helped pave the way for the modern superhero boom. From Wesley Snipes’ Blade to Tobey Maguire’s Spider-Man and Ben Affleck’s Daredevil, this is a deep dive into Marvel before Iron Man and the MCU era began.
If you love Marvel movies, superhero movie discussions, comic book films, or nostalgic movie conversations, this episode is for you.

entry.title: [MK] The Punisher: One Last Kill | Special Presentation Review | Marvel
entry.summary:
The Punisher: One Last Kill review! We break down Marvel’s new Special Presentation starring Jon Bernthal as Frank Castle and discuss whether this brutal return delivers for Punisher fans.
Marvel brings Frank Castle back in The Punisher: One Last Kill, a dark and violent Special Presentation set in the MCU. We discuss the action, performances, story, MCU connections, and whether this feels like the Punisher fans have been waiting for since the The Punisher Netflix era.
Does Marvel finally let the Punisher go full Punisher again? And where could Frank Castle appear next in the MCU?
If you love Marvel reviews, MCU discussions, comic book movies, and movie reviews, you’re in the right place.

entry.title: [MK] What’s the Best Video Game Movie Ever Made? | The Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Video game movies used to have a terrible reputation… but not anymore. In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we break down our favorite video game movies, from the Sonic franchise and The Super Mario Bros. Movie to Detective Pikachu, Mortal Kombat, Need for Speed, and more.
We talk about why video game adaptations are finally working, which movies surprised us the most, and which gaming franchises still deserve better adaptations. Plus, we discuss the upcoming Battlefield movie, the state of Hollywood gaming adaptations, and some underrated picks that deserve more love.
If you love video game movies, gaming adaptations, movie discussions, or film podcasts, this episode is for you.

In this video I walk through all of it. The collectibles, the movie-themed pieces that mean the most to me, the best Blu-rays and special editions in my collection, and the setup I use every single day.

entry.title: [MK] These Unexpected Gems Blew Our Minds | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Some movies completely surprise you.
In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we talk about movies we didn’t expect to like — whether it was bad trailers, weak marketing, or preconceived opinions — but ended up loving anyway.
From unexpected hits to movies that completely exceeded expectations, we break down what changed our minds and why these films worked better than we thought.
If you’re into movie discussions, film debates, and discovering underrated or surprising movies, this episode is for you.

entry.title: [MK] The Devil Wears Prada 2 | Movie Review
entry.summary:
The Devil Wears Prada 2 review! Is the long-awaited sequel worth it? We break down the performances, story, and whether this fashion classic follow-up delivers or falls apart.
After 20 years, The Devil Wears Prada 2 finally arrives—and it’s surprisingly charming. With returning stars like Anne Hathaway, Meryl Streep, Emily Blunt, and Stanley Tucci, this legacy sequel brings strong performances, sharp comedy, and great chemistry—but a messy third act holds it back from greatness.
We break down what works, what doesn’t, and whether this sequel lives up to the original classic.
Review Timestamps: 00:00 – Intro 00:25 – Expectations & Sequel Setup 01:27 – Thoughts on the Original 02:16 – Story & Andy’s Return 03:11 – Performances & Chemistry 03:39 – Third Act Issues 04:55 – Emotional Core & Themes 06:25 – Standout Performances 06:56 – Meryl Streep’s Character Arc 08:27 – Comedy & Overall Enjoyment 09:22 – Final Thoughts & Rating 11:06 – Outro
🎬 More from The Movie Knights: All Links: https://linktr.ee/Movie_Knights
Be sure to like, subscribe, and stick around for more movie reviews.
#devilwearsprada #moviereview #movierecommendation #movies

entry.title: [MK] The Sheep Detectives | Movie Review
entry.summary:
The Sheep Detectives review — this new family comedy mystery had potential, but does it actually deliver or fall flat?
In this review, we break down the story, humor, and performances, and why this one feels more like a basic kids movie than something with wider appeal. There are a few fun moments, but overall it leaves a lot to be desired—especially considering the talent behind it.
Is The Sheep Detectives worth watching or an easy skip?

entry.title: [MK] These Sequels Are BETTER Than The Original | The Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Sometimes the sequel is actually better than the original.
In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we break down sequels that surpassed the original movies — from iconic follow-ups like The Dark Knight and The Godfather Part II to fan-favorite sequels like Spider-Man 2, Terminator 2: Judgment Day, and more.
We discuss what makes a great sequel, why some second installments improve on the original, and which franchises peaked with their follow-ups.
If you’re looking for the best movie sequels, film debates, and movie discussions, this episode has you covered.

entry.title: [MK] Michael | Movie Review
entry.summary:
Michael (2026) review – the Michael Jackson biopic directed by Antoine Fuqua and starring Jaafar Jackson. Does this highly anticipated music biopic live up to the King of Pop?
In this review, we break down the performances, story, pacing, and why this film is a mixed bag. It’s incredibly entertaining, fast-paced, and packed with iconic music—but the story feels surface-level and plays it safe.
Is Michael worth watching or just another standard musical biopic?

entry.title: [MK] Lee Cronin's The Mummy | Movie Review
entry.summary:
The Mummy (2026), directed by Lee Cronin (Evil Dead Rise), delivers a brutal, gore-heavy new take on the classic monster—but does it actually work?
In this review, we break down the story, performances, scares, and why this one feels like a mix of great horror moments and frustrating storytelling. The third act goes HARD, but pacing issues, weak performances, and an identity crisis hold it back.
Is The Mummy worth watching or just another “meh” reboot?
Movie Review Timestamps: 00:00 – The Mummy (2026) Review & Premise 00:26 – First Impressions: Very Mixed 00:56 – What Works: Fresh Take & Big Swings 01:48 – Insane Third Act, Gore & Horror Highlights 02:17 – Family Story & Emotional Core 02:47 – Weak Performances (Adults vs Kids) 03:16 – Pacing Problems & Runtime Issues 03:44 – Identity Crisis: Evil Dead vs Mummy 05:12 – More Positives: Scares, Tension & Set Pieces 06:13 – Story Issues & Narrative Problems 08:15 – Ending, Pacing & Final Stretch Issues 09:12 – Final Rating & Verdict (2.5 Stars)

entry.title: [MK] Movies That SHOULD Be Remade | The Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Hollywood loves remakes… but they’re usually the wrong ones.
In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we break down movies that SHOULD be remade — films with great concepts, flawed execution, or untapped potential that deserve a modern update.
From cult classics like Big Trouble in Little China to high-concept sci-fi like In Time, we discuss what worked, what didn’t, and how these movies could be reimagined for today’s audience.
If you’re into movie discussions, film debates, and breaking down Hollywood trends, this is the episode for you.
Episode Timestamps: 00:00 - Movies That SHOULD Be Remade 00:30 - Why Hollywood Gets Remakes Wrong 01:05 - Anaconda & The Right Way to Do a Remake 02:50 - Big Trouble in Little China Remake Potential 06:01 - In Time Deserves a Second Chance 09:30 - The Three Amigos Modern Remake Idea 13:17 - Eddie and the Cruisers Revisit 15:45 - Should Stand By Me Be Remade? 18:38 - Eyes Without a Face Horror Remake Potential 20:09 - Game: Remake Edition
🎬 More from The Movie Knights: All Links: https://linktr.ee/Movie_Knights
Be sure to like, subscribe, and stick around for more movie reviews!
#movies #remakes #moviediscussion

entry.title: [MK] These Movies Should NOT Have Gotten A Sequel | The Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Some movies should have been left alone… but Hollywood couldn’t help itself.
In this episode of The Movie Knights Roundtable, we break down movies that should NOT have gotten sequels. From controversial follow-ups like Joker 2 to unnecessary comedy sequels, franchise overextensions, and legacy cash grabs that missed the point of the original.
We dive into what makes a bad sequel, the biggest mistakes studios make when continuing stories, and which films actually hurt their legacy by getting another installment. Plus, we pitch some wild sequel ideas and play a game to wrap it all up.
If you love movie discussions, film debates, and breaking down Hollywood trends, this is the episode for you.
Episode Timestamps:
00:00 - Movies That Should NOT Have Gotten Sequels 00:30 - Why Hollywood Keeps Making Unnecessary Sequels 01:52 - Joker & The Sequel Debate 06:44 - Why Comedy Sequels Usually Fail 14:58 - Percy Jackson Franchise Missteps 18:58 - What’s the Biggest Sequel Mistake? 20:11 - Why Toy Story 4 Actually Works 21:27 - The Mask Sequel Disaster 21:50 - More Movies That Didn’t Need Sequels 23:40 - Fixing Jaws Sequels (Soft Pitch) 26:31 - Game: Sequel Edition 27:24 - Air Bud Mockumentary Pitch
🎬 More from The Movie Knights: All Links: https://linktr.ee/Movie_Knights
Be sure to like, subscribe, and stick around for more movie reviews!
#movies #sequels #moviediscussion

entry.title: [MK] Best Movies of the 2020s Ranked (Oscar Nominees Bracket) | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:
Best movies of the 2020s ranked using every Best Picture nominee in a March Madness-style bracket. In this episode, we debate and break down the top Oscar-nominated films of the decade, from major winners to underrated contenders, to determine which movie truly stands above the rest.
We compare award winners, surprise nominees, and fan-favorite films while discussing cultural impact, performances, directing, and what defines the best movie of the 2020s so far.
If you’re looking for the best movies of the 2020s, Oscar nominees ranked, or a full breakdown of Academy Award contenders, this bracket delivers some of the most fun and heated movie debates we’ve had.

entry.title: [MK] The Super Mario Galaxy Movie | Movie Review
entry.summary:
In our review of Super Mario Galaxy, Nintendo takes Mario to space in a vibrant, fast-paced animated adventure. We break down the animation, humor, voice performances, and how this film expands the Mario universe. It’s a crowd-pleasing family movie with plenty for longtime fans, but does it live up to the hype?
Mario Movie Review Timestamps: 00:00 – Super Mario Galaxy Movie Review & First Impressions 00:50 – Better Than the First Movie? 01:43 – Cast, Animation & What Works 03:07 – Story Issues & Bowser’s Arc 04:53 – New Characters: Yoshi, Bowser Jr. & More 05:20 – Pacing Problems & Abrupt Ending 05:49 – Yoshi STEALS the Movie 06:15 – Fan Service: Hits & Misses 07:14 – Final Thoughts & Verdict 08:03 – Rating & Post-Credit Scenes
🎬 More from The Movie Knights: All Links: https://linktr.ee/Movie_Knights
Be sure to like, subscribe, and stick around for more movie reviews!
#SuperMarioGalaxy #Nintendo #MovieReview #videogames

entry.title: [JR] Movie Collecting Doesn’t Feel The Same Anymore…
entry.summary:
For a long time, movie collecting was about the hunt. Digging through shelves. Stumbling on a hidden gem. Finding something rare for a great price when you least expected it.
That feeling is mostly gone.
Today, collecting feels like endless online shopping. Limited editions announced months in advance. Preorders, countdown timers, and “out of stock” notices within minutes. Scalpers and resellers are flipping everything before fans even have a chance.
In this video, I talk about how physical media collecting has changed, why the thrill of the hunt has disappeared, and how the hobby has shifted from discovery to mindless cart-filling. What we gained, what we lost, and whether there’s any way to get that magic back.
If you’ve ever missed the feeling of finding something special on a shelf, this one’s for you.

For a long time, movie collecting was about the hunt. Digging through shelves. Stumbling on a hidden gem. Finding something rare for a great price when you least expected it.

That feeling is mostly gone.

Today, collecting feels like endless online shopping. Limited editions announced months in advance. Preorders, countdown timers, and “out of stock” notices within minutes. Scalpers and resellers are flipping everything before fans even have a chance.

In this video, I talk about how physical media collecting has changed, why the thrill of the hunt has disappeared, and how the hobby has shifted from discovery to mindless cart-filling. What we gained, what we lost, and whether there’s any way to get that magic back.

If you’ve ever missed the feeling of finding something special on a shelf, this one’s for you.

entry.title: [JR] Mini-LED vs OLED in 2026 | Sony Bravia 9 4K TV Review
entry.summary:
In this video, I review the Sony Bravia 9 Mini-LED 4K TV after a couple months of real world use in my home theater. Sony positions the Bravia 9 as its flagship Mini-LED television, aimed at viewers who want OLED-level performance without the typical OLED trade-offs. So are they right?
I break down how the Bravia 9 performs with movies, TV shows, gaming, and everyday viewing, focusing on brightness, contrast, local dimming, motion handling, and upscaling. I also compare Mini-LED vs OLED where it actually matters, not just on a spec sheet.
This is not a first-impressions video. It’s a practical look at whether the Sony Bravia 9 is worth buying if you’re shopping for a high-end 4K TV in 2026.
Topics covered: • Mini-LED vs OLED real-world performance • Brightness, contrast, and blooming control • 4K HDR performance (Blu-ray movies and streaming) • Who this TV is for and who should skip it

entry.content:
In this video, I review the Sony Bravia 9 Mini-LED 4K TV after a couple months of real world use in my home theater. Sony positions the Bravia 9 as its flagship Mini-LED television, aimed at viewers who want OLED-level performance without the typical OLED trade-offs. So are they right?

I break down how the Bravia 9 performs with movies, TV shows, gaming, and everyday viewing, focusing on brightness, contrast, local dimming, motion handling, and upscaling. I also compare Mini-LED vs OLED where it actually matters, not just on a spec sheet.

This is not a first-impressions video. It’s a practical look at whether the Sony Bravia 9 is worth buying if you’re shopping for a high-end 4K TV in 2026.

Topics covered:
• Mini-LED vs OLED real-world performance
• Brightness, contrast, and blooming control
• 4K HDR performance (Blu-ray movies and streaming)
• Who this TV is for and who should skip it

entry.title: [JR] What Netflix Buying Warner Brothers Means for Physical Media
entry.summary:
Netflix winning the bid to buy Warner Brothers is a massive moment for Hollywood, but the biggest question for collectors is simple. What happens to physical media if this deal goes through?
Warner’s home entertainment division is currently part of the SDS joint venture with Universal, and that partnership controls everything from 4K discs to Blu rays to GRUV. If Netflix takes over WB, that entire ecosystem could change overnight. Netflix has never cared about discs. They’ve avoided physical media entirely unless a director forces it. So does this deal put the future of Warner’s catalog at risk? Could boutique labels like Criterion, Arrow, Kino Lorber, Vinegar Syndrome, and others end up becoming the real home for these movies? Or does SDS survive the corporate shakeup?
In this video I break down what this acquisition could mean for collectors, theatrical windows, boutique labels, and the long term outlook for owning movies in a world where streaming giants want everything behind a subscription.
If you care about 4K, Blu ray, steelbooks, boutique labels, or the future of film preservation, this is the video you want to watch.

entry.title: [MK] V for Vendetta Returns — HBO Is Taking a Huge Swing | Movie Knights Roundtable
entry.summary:

entry.title: [JR] This App Has Better Movies Than Most Paid Services
entry.summary:
Kanopy might be the best free streaming option that way too many movie fans overlook. In this video, I walk through how to sign up, what you need to access it, and why its catalog quietly rivals the paid services.
You only need a library card, and once you are in you get ad free streaming, true cinema focused curation, and a library of classic films, award winners, indie gems, international releases, documentaries, and more. This is not another free catalog full of filler. This is the good stuff.
Head to Kanopy,com to check if your library or university participates and sign-up today!

This is not a ranking or a list of the greatest movies ever made. Just a personal look at the films that left fingerprints on who I am as a movie fan and a person.

entry.title: [JR] Chris Stuckmann Made a Movie. Let’s Talk About Shelby Oaks
entry.summary:
Chris Stuckmann releasing Shelby Oak is a big deal, not just for him as a filmmaker, but for creators who grew up on this platform and are pushing beyond it.
This video is part discussion about Shelby Oaks and part conversation about what happens when content creators move into new creative worlds. I talk about the film, the reactions to it, the challenge of reviewing work from someone who comes from the same space, and why some people will never accept YouTubers who step outside the platform.
I also reflect on my own journey as a creator, writing my book, and thinking about long term creative goals beyond YouTube. Building a community here is powerful, but it can also be the launchpad to bigger creative ambitions. Watching Chris take this leap feels like a natural evolution of the creator space, and it is exciting to see.

entry.content:
Chris Stuckmann releasing Shelby Oak is a big deal, not just for him as a filmmaker, but for creators who grew up on this platform and are pushing beyond it.

This video is part discussion about Shelby Oaks and part conversation about what happens when content creators move into new creative worlds. I talk about the film, the reactions to it, the challenge of reviewing work from someone who comes from the same space, and why some people will never accept YouTubers who step outside the platform.

I also reflect on my own journey as a creator, writing my book, and thinking about long term creative goals beyond YouTube. Building a community here is powerful, but it can also be the launchpad to bigger creative ambitions. Watching Chris take this leap feels like a natural evolution of the creator space, and it is exciting to see.

entry.title: [JR] The Best Horror Movie Sequels Ever Made
entry.summary:
Horror sequels usually crash and burn…but not these. These are the rare few that matched the original, or even outdid it.
Not all horror sequels are created equal — but some are just as legendary as the originals. In this video, I’m breaking down the best horror movie sequels of all time, from classic slashers to modern masterpieces that prove great horror doesn’t stop after the first film.
We’ll talk about Friday the 13th: The Final Chapter, A Nightmare on Elm Street 3: Dream Warriors, The Conjuring 2, Doctor Sleep, and The Exorcist III — five sequels that became essential horror viewing.
These films show how horror can evolve, surprise, and even surpass the original. Whether it’s the brutal energy of Friday the 13th Part 4, the creativity of Dream Warriors, or the haunting atmosphere of The Conjuring 2 and Doctor Sleep, these movies prove that horror sequels can be just as powerful as the original, and sometimes, even better.
If you’re a horror fan, collector, or just love revisiting classic franchises, this list is for you.

entry.title: [JR] Master and Commander 4K UHD Blu-ray Review | Is the Audio Really That Bad?
entry.summary:
In this review, I break down the newly released Master and Commander: The Far Side of the World on 4K Ultra HD Blu-ray. From the jaw-dropping visuals and Dolby Atmos sound to the disc’s special features, packaging, and transfer quality, find out if this is a must-own for your physical media collection. Plus, what's up with that bass? We will dive in!
Directed by Peter Weir and starring Russell Crowe and Paul Bettany, Master and Commander is a modern naval epic that deserves a high-end presentation. But does this 4K Blu-ray deliver?
Whether you’re a collector or just upgrading from DVD or Blu-ray, this review will help you decide if Master and Commander deserves a spot on your shelf.
